message:
test(fuzz): add read/write/pread/pwrite/readv/writev harness (bd-sttk9)

Exercises frankenlibc_abi::unistd_abi::{read,write} and
frankenlibc_abi::io_abi::{pread,pwrite,readv,writev} against five
pre-opened deterministic backends (/dev/zero, /dev/null, a
pipe2() pair, a force-closed fd, and a wild -1/INT_MAX/2 fd).
The fuzzer picks an op, fd, buffer size, offset, and iovec layout
each iteration and invokes our wrapper.

Sentinel-byte invariant: every buffer window is padded with 64B
of 0xAA sentinel bytes before and after; any tampered sentinel
means the wrapper wrote/read past the requested count — which is
the canonical buffer-overrun signature. Enforced for both the
scalar read/write path and the vectored readv/writev path.

Differential oracle: for the four non-pipe backends the harness
replays the identical call through libc::{read,write,pread,pwrite}
and asserts bit-for-bit agreement on success/failure and on
byte-count. Pipe backends are skipped because our writes to them
are observable state that would bias the host side's next read.

Performance: 108K exec/s on an 8-core VM (16.3M execs in 151s
with zero crashes). Well above the 1000 exec/s floor in
/testing-fuzzing Hard Rule 1.

Registered as fuzz_read_write in crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/Cargo.toml
and seeded with the corpus harvested during the fuzz pass that
caught two harness-side bugs (count > buf_size overrun, buf_offset
shifting the window into post-pad) before finding any real impl
bugs — both fixed before commit.

Parent epic: bd-jy4qu.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -125,6 +125,11 @@ name = "fuzz_open_syscalls"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_open_syscalls.rs"
 doc = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_read_write"
+path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_read_write.rs"
+doc = false
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "fuzz_strftime"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_strftime.rs"
